identity.name: Class
identity.definition: A Class is an organizational grouping of students under one or more teachers for a specific subject, grade level, or instructional period that serves as the primary unit for assigning work, administering assessments, viewing reports, and managing day-to-day instruction across Renaissance products.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Class appears in grids and dashboards

attributes.name: Class Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Class is shown.

attributes.name: Teacher
attributes.description: The primary teacher assigned to the class.

attributes.name: Grade Level
attributes.description: The grade level of the class. PreK-12, UG.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: The subject area taught in this class.

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Number of students currently enrolled in the class.

attributes.name: Class Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears everywhere a Class is shown.

attributes.name: Teacher
attributes.description: The primary teacher assigned to the class.

attributes.name: Student Roster
attributes.description: Students currently enrolled in the class.

attributes.name: Grade Level
attributes.description: The grade level of the class. PreK-12, UG.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: The subject area taught in this class.

attributes.name: School
attributes.description: The school this class belongs to.

attributes.name: School Year
attributes.description: The academic year this class is active for.

attributes.name: Period / Block
attributes.description: The scheduling period or block for this class.

attributes.name: Student Count
attributes.description: Number of students currently enrolled in the class.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Active or Archived. Controls visibility in dashboards and reports.

attributes.name: Product Settings
attributes.description: Per-product configuration for this class (e.g., reading level range, practice settings).

attributes.name: Co-Teacher(s)
attributes.description: Additional teachers who share responsibility for this class.

attributes.name: SIS Section ID
attributes.description: External SIS identifier for cross-system matching and roster sync.

stories.title: View Class Roster
stories.benefit: I can see all enrolled students, their current levels, and quickly assign work or schedule assessments for the group.
stories.whenClause: I open a Class from my dashboard,
stories.thenClause: the full student roster with key attributes, active assignments, and recent scores for the class.

stories.title: Assign Work to Class
stories.benefit: all students in the class receive the work simultaneously without individual assignment.
stories.whenClause: I create an assignment or schedule an assessment for a class,
stories.thenClause: every active student in the class roster should receive the assignment and it should appear in their to-do lists.

stories.title: View Class Report
stories.benefit: I can identify trends, compare student performance, and adjust instruction for the group.
stories.thenClause: aggregated scores, growth data, and performance distributions across all students in the class.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Class is uniquely identified within a School by its name plus school year. The SIS Section ID provides cross-system matching.

businessRules.title: Creation
businessRules.description: Classes are primarily created via SIS roster sync (OneRoster, Clever, ClassLink). Manual creation requires at minimum: Class Name, Teacher, Grade Level, School.

businessRules.title: Teacher Requirement
businessRules.description: A Class must have at least one primary Teacher assigned. Co-Teachers are optional. A Teacher may be assigned to multiple Classes.

businessRules.title: Enrollment Limits
businessRules.description: A Class has no hard system limit on student enrollment, but product-specific caps may apply (e.g., assessment session limits). Typical range: 15-35 students.

businessRules.title: Year Binding
businessRules.description: A Class is bound to a single School Year. At year-end, classes are archived and new classes are created for the next year via SIS sync or manual rollover.

businessRules.title: Data Isolation
businessRules.description: Class-level data (assignments, scores, reports) is scoped to the class. Teachers see only their assigned classes. Admins see all classes within their school.

businessRules.title: Archive vs Delete
businessRules.description: Archiving a Class hides it from active views but preserves all historical data. Deletion permanently removes the class record (Admin only) and orphans associated data.

states.name: Active
states.description: Currently in use for instruction and assignments

states.name: Archived
states.description: No longer active but data preserved for historical reporting

relationships.mechanics: Students are enrolled in Classes primarily via SIS roster sync (automated). Teachers and Admins can also manually add/remove students.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Class contains multiple Students; a Student can belong to multiple Classes. Typical: 15-35 students per class, 1-8 classes per student.
relationships.sorts: Students within a Class: alphabetical by last name (default), by grade level, by reading/math level.
relationships.filters: By enrollment status (active/inactive), by proficiency level, by demographic group.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, students remain in the system but lose their class association for that class. Historical scores and assignments persist.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers are assigned to Classes via SIS sync or Admin action. A Class must have at least one primary Teacher; Co-Teachers are optional.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Class has one primary Teacher and zero or more Co-Teachers; a Teacher can teach multiple Classes.
relationships.sorts: Classes for a Teacher: alphabetical by name (default), by grade level, by subject.
relationships.filters: By subject, by grade level, by school year.
relationships.dependencies: If a Teacher is deactivated, their classes must be reassigned to another Teacher before the teacher record can be fully removed.

relationships.mechanics: Teachers and Admins schedule Assessments for a Class. All active students in the class are included in the assessment session.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. A Class can have multiple Assessments scheduled; an Assessment can target multiple Classes.
relationships.sorts: Assessments for a Class: by date (upcoming first, default), by type, by subject.
relationships.filters: By assessment type (Star, DnA, FastBridge), by subject, by date range, by completion status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Class is archived, scheduled future assessments are cancelled. Completed assessment results persist.

nestedObjects.name: Student

nestedObjects.name: Teacher

nestedObjects.name: Resource
nestedObjects.description: Learning resources assigned or recommended for this class

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: Assessment scores generated by students in this class

structure.evidence: A Class has a rich set of attributes: name, teacher, grade level, subject, school, school year, period/block, student roster, co-teachers, status, product settings, and SIS Section ID. You can design a detail page for a Class.

instances.evidence: 3rd Grade ELA - Period 1 (Ms. Rodriguez, Lincoln Elementary), 7th Grade Math Block A (Mr. Chen, Washington Middle), AP Biology Section 2 (Dr. Patel, Jefferson High). Every school has dozens to hundreds of Class instances.

purpose.evidence: Classes are the primary operational container for instruction. Teachers assign work to Classes, schedule assessments for Classes, and view reports by Class. Students experience their day organized by Class.

sipValidation.verdict: Core system object. The primary operational container for instruction.

synonyms.notes: Standard term in OneRoster and Clever integrations
Sometimes used loosely; distinct from formal Student Groups
Often used interchangeably with class in casual conversation

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Classes a teacher is responsible for, shown as cards with student count and active assignment status. The starting point for creating assignments, scheduling assessments, and viewing class reports.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What classes do I have, and what's their current status?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: All classes across a school with teacher, enrollment, and performance metrics in a data table. Admins compare class performance, attendance, and spot patterns across the roster.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: How are all classes performing across my school?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A student's enrolled classes shown as compact cards with teacher name and upcoming due dates. Students check what's due next and navigate to class content.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What classes am I in, and what's due?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: The complete class record — roster, schedule, product settings, and full administrative controls. Teachers and admins manage enrollment, create assignments, schedule assessments, and view reports from here.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What is the full detail of this class?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A compact selection row for choosing which class to schedule an assessment for. Shows grade level, student count, and eligibility to help teachers pick the right target.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: Which class should I schedule this assessment for?

eligibleStudentCount.description: Students eligible to take the assessment

lastAssessmentDate.description: When the class was last assessed

shapeshifterMatrix.description: The class displayed as a banner at the top of a report, identifying which class the results below apply to. No interactive elements.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What class is this report about?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A teacher's full class list with grade, subject, period, and student counts. Teachers check their schedule and jump into specific classes to manage assignments.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What classes do I teach and what's the student breakdown?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Classes shown as nested cards within a student's profile, with embedded assignment and assessment info. Teachers see everything a student is enrolled in and what they're working on.
shapeshifterMatrix.cardShape: Nested card with embedded assignments and assessments
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What classes is this student enrolled in?
